cityDNA.coreFantasy: An old grey-stone Celtic river-market capital where oaths, craft, archives, tenements, theater, and hillfort memory are made into public life.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Dun Liath is not wilderness nostalgia; it is public oath, market speech, archive memory, craft legitimacy, low residential density, and communal ceremony made into stone.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Dun Liath turns knotwork registry law, covered bazaars, artisan guilds, archives, tenement courts, theater culture, and the green spiral hillfort into a settled river civilization of oaths, memory, trade, craft, and performance.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Grey stone, mossy masonry, dry-stone paving, dark timber, red awnings, blue lamps, round towers, covered bazaars, guildhalls, archive towers, tenement courts, opera courts, bridge streets, hillfort rings, and awninged public markets.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: An Irish/Celtic-coded civic river capital where market speech, public records, guild craft, performance, and communal oath bind urban life.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Spiral knotwork, oath rings, round towers, gold seals, red awnings, blue lamps, woven banners, standing-circle silhouettes, masks, auction courts, masterwork arcades, and laundry wells symbolize public memory.

canonLocks: Dun Liath is an old Irish/Celtic civic river town scaled into a capital.
The Wonder is the Oath-Ring Hillfort of the Green Spiral.
The Seat of Power is the Spiral Knotwork Sovereign Registry Court.
The Beacon is the Covered Bazaar Beacon.
The five primary districts are Grand Market, Artisan Guilds, Records / Archives, Low Residential, and Entertainment / Theater.

The uploaded district anchors include Grand Market Civic Hall, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, Low Residential Lantern Monument, Laundry Well Nook, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, and Mask Pavilion Nook.
provisionalCanon: Dun Liath's highest civic virtue is witnessed oath.
The central tension is public memory versus public performance: truth and theater both happen in front of the same community.
The Masterwork Arcade likely functions as craft proof, social prestige, and guild politics in one place.
rejectedReads: Dun Liath is a wild Celtic ruin.
Dun Liath is only a market town without civic authority.
Theater and performance are decorative rather than civic-social forces.
Guild craft and archive memory can be smoothed into generic medieval flavor.
cautionFlags: Keep the city dense, lived-in, and permanent.
Do not over-romanticize wilderness; this is a civic river capital.
Preserve the mix of market, archive, guild, residential, and performance life.
Keep exact sidecar anchors like Awning Monument and Masterwork Arcade Nook visible, because they sharpen Dun Liath's market and craft identity.

climateLighting: soft golden-hour light on grey stone
blue civic lamp and beacon glow
environment.environmentalLogic: River, market, bridge, archive, guild, tenement, theater, and hillfort all interlock. Public routes carry speech, trade, oaths, craft proof, and performance through dense stone civic space.

skyline: Spiral Knotwork Sovereign Registry Court as central civic authority
Covered Bazaar Beacon as market-rooted signal landmark
Oath-Ring Hillfort of the Green Spiral as far ritual/civic anchor
archive towers, guild towers, market roofs, opera structures, and bridge-linked civic terraces
round-tower and spire language linking city to Celtic monastic and hillfort precedent

civilization.people: Citizens are public, verbal, oath-conscious, craft-conscious, archive-aware, and comfortable with market argument and theater as shared civic life.
civilization.governance: Authority is oath-registry based, expressed through spiral knotwork records, public markets, guild legitimacy, archives, and communal witnessing.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is woven through oath rings, hillfort memory, standing-circle silhouettes, public ceremony, and communal obligations rather than separate temple dominance.
customs: public satire that tests memory and reputation
bodyLanguage: tenement residents watching from windows and wells

streets.appearance: Dense grey-stone streets with bridges, red awnings, round towers, market canopies, guild fronts, masterwork arcades, archive stairs, laundry wells, tenement courts, and theater lanterns.
streets.movement: Movement follows bridge streets, market courts, guild arcades, archive stairs, tenement alleys, opera plazas, and hill roads toward the oath-ring wonder.
streets.lighting: Blue civic light and warm market or theater lanterns contrast with cool grey stone and moss.
streets.weatherEffects: Rain darkens stone, brightens moss, fills market awnings with sound, and makes bridge streets reflective.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime is market, craft, archive, and bridge traffic; evening shifts toward theater, tavern-like warmth, and public performance.
streets.nightCycle: Night is blue lamps, red awning lanterns, theater glow, river reflections, and close tenement sound.

housing.wealthy: Registry- and guild-adjacent stone houses with carved knotwork, private courtyards, blue lamps, and views toward bridges or hill roads.
housing.middle: Guild family homes, rooms over shops, bridge-street apartments, red awnings, shared courts, and dark timber interiors.
housing.poor: Dense tenement courts, laundry-well rooms, market-edge flats, mossy stone lanes, shared water, and close public sound.

districts.name: Grand Market
districts.civicPurpose: Public trade, speech, bargaining, assembly, auction, and Beacon-centered civic warning.
districts.visualIdentity: Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, market canopies, red awnings, bridge traffic, and blue lamps.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Warm, loud, public, and socially binding.

districts.name: Artisan Guilds
districts.civicPurpose: Craft legitimacy, apprenticeship, production, guild rank, and public proof of skilled work.
districts.visualIdentity: Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, workshop fronts, tools, and craft displays.

districts.name: Records / Archives
districts.civicPurpose: Archive memory, public record, oath preservation, and legal continuity.
districts.visualIdentity: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, stairs, cool light, and quiet courts.

districts.name: Low Residential
districts.civicPurpose: Dense ordinary life, domestic continuity, neighborhood gossip, shared services, and communal witness at close range.
districts.visualIdentity: Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, Laundry Well Nook, narrow lanes, mossy walls, blue lamps, and red fabric.

districts.name: Entertainment / Theater
districts.civicPurpose: Public performance, communal ceremony, satire, memory, social release, and masked civic commentary.
districts.visualIdentity: Entertainment / Theater Civic Hall, Opera Court, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, Mask Pavilion Nook, stages, banners, crowd terraces, and theater lanterns.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Bright, dramatic, communal, and slightly dangerous.

wonder.name: Oath-Ring Hillfort of the Green Spiral
wonder.symbolicRole: Communal oath, old hill memory, and civic legitimacy outside the market core.
wonder.visualRole: Far hillfort ring with green spiral identity, stone forms, ritual high ground, and visible continuity with the city's oath culture.
wonder.civicRole: Wonder-scale anchor for oaths, public memory, ancient legitimacy, and the city's relationship to hillfort precedent.
storyHooks: an oath at the hillfort changes a market dispute into civic law
a performance retells the oath differently than the archive does

beacon.name: Covered Bazaar Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Warning rooted in public market life.
beacon.visualRole: Beacon rising from covered bazaar awnings, Grand Market civic space, red canopies, and blue-lit public routes.
beacon.civicRole: Signal heart for market assembly, public notice, auction interruption, and citywide reaction.
storyHooks: a warning interrupts a public auction
the market interprets the Beacon faster than the registry can issue a notice

seatOfPower.name: Spiral Knotwork Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Oath and record interlaced into law.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central registry with spiral knotwork, round-tower forms, gold seals, civic terraces, and public approaches from market and archive districts.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for records, oaths, guild legitimacy, public memory, and legal continuity.
storyHooks: a knotwork record exposes an oath someone wants forgotten
a registry ruling makes a theater satire dangerous

majorLandmarks.name: Guildhall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Workshop hall, woven banners, tools, masterwork arcade, craft displays, and guild approaches.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Guild certification, apprenticeship, craft legitimacy, and public proof of skilled work.
storyHooks: a masterwork proves a claim the archive cannot
a guild banner marks who may speak in a dispute

majorLandmarks.name: Archive Tower
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Memory raised, sealed, copied, and cited.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Archive tower with copyist hall, gold-seal monument, quiet stairs, cool light, and civic record massing.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Preserves oaths, legal memory, public records, and continuity between hillfort past and city present.
storyHooks: a copyist finds a phrase everyone quotes wrong
an old oath record changes a modern market conflict

majorLandmarks.name: Tenement Court
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Dense residential court with laundry well, lantern monument, narrow stairs, mossy walls, families, and red fabric.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Anchors domestic continuity, shared services, gossip, and the lived consequences of public rulings.
storyHooks: a laundry-well rumor reaches the market before officials do
a tenement court remembers an oath no archive cites

majorLandmarks.name: Opera Court
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Performance as public memory, satire, and sanctioned pressure.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Theater court with mask pavilion, lantern monument, stages, banners, crowd terraces, and warm performance light.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Hosts public performance, communal ceremony, satire, and social release.
storyHooks: a satire changes how the city remembers an oath
a mask pavilion meeting becomes more public than intended

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, red awnings, market stalls, crowds, and blue lamps.
S2 must show Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, tools, workshops, apprentices, and public craft displays.
S3 must show Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, stairs, clerks, and sealed records.
S4 must show Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, Laundry Well Nook, shared stairs, families, and dense residential life.
S5 must show Entertainment / Theater Civic Hall, Opera Court, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, Mask Pavilion Nook, stage, performers, lanterns, and audience tiers.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Use a dense grey-stone Celtic river capital with bridges, red awnings, round towers, covered bazaars, guildhalls, archive towers, tenement courts, opera courts, and the green spiral hillfort beyond.

The Oath-Ring Hillfort of the Green Spiral should be the far wonder anchor; the Spiral Knotwork Sovereign Registry Court should be central authority; the Covered Bazaar Beacon should tie warning to market life.
Preserve the mix of public oath, market speech, craft proof, archives, low residential witness, and performance.
requiredVisualElements: Oath-Ring Hillfort of the Green Spiral
elementsToAvoid: empty stone city without dense public life
guild craft omitted from civic authority
archives replaced by vague memory motifs
low residential areas treated as background filler
